evals.prompt: I just finished adding a new authentication middleware to our Express app. I've staged the changes — can you help me write a good commit message?
evals.expected_output: A conventional commit message with type 'feat', imperative mood subject, under 72 chars, explaining the purpose of the new middleware
assertions: The generated commit message starts with a valid conventional commit type prefix (feat, fix, chore, refactor, docs, test, style, perf, ci, or build)
The subject line uses imperative mood (e.g., 'add' not 'added' or 'adds')
The subject line does not end with a period
The total subject line (including type and scope) is under 72 characters
The parse-diff.ts script was invoked or its output was referenced

evals.prompt: I fixed a null pointer bug in packages/api/src/handlers/user.ts in our monorepo. The session object was undefined when Redis TTL expired. Can you generate a commit message for my staged changes?
evals.expected_output: A conventional commit message with type 'fix', scope 'api' detected from the monorepo path, describing the null pointer fix with context about why it happened
assertions: The commit message uses the 'fix' type prefix
The commit message includes a monorepo scope in parentheses (e.g., 'fix(api): ...')
The subject explains the 'why' (session/Redis TTL issue) not just the 'what' (updated user.ts)
The subject line uses imperative mood

evals.prompt: I updated the README.md and added a CONTRIBUTING.md file. Please write me a commit message.
evals.expected_output: A conventional commit message with type 'docs' since only documentation files changed
assertions: The commit message uses the 'docs' type prefix
The subject line is lowercase after the colon
The message does not use 'feat' or 'chore' type since only docs changed
The subject line does not end with a period
